governingIntent: V1 (maldous/react) is the complete reference for V2. Reuse/move/refine/wrap proven V1 data assets. Zero discovery. No legacy/temp/transitional naming in the final tree. DO NOT modify any product/runtime file; this artefact is read-only-derived and written only under docs/v2-foundation/.
scope: PostgreSQL (platform DB, schema-per-tenant), Redis, ClickHouse, MinIO/object-storage, plus migration chain, RLS, seeds/fixtures, backups, restore, destructive-op controls. Keycloak-postgres / Sentry-* / Sonar-postgres / Loki / Grafana / pgAdmin volumes are dependency-stack stores, not platform product data — retained as-is, listed under externalVolumes.

summary.canonicalStore: PostgreSQL 16.6 (image postgres:16.6-alpine), database 'platform', single shared instance in dev/test. Local-first: every USF capability substrate (metering, search, event-bus, observability, secrets, scheduled-jobs) is delivered as a Postgres table first; ClickHouse/Meilisearch/Redis-streams/OpenBao etc. are provider adapters behind ports, NOT additional schema owners.
summary.migrationModel: Hand-rolled forward-only runner (apps/platform-api/src/db/migrate.ts). Numbered SQL files 001..034 in apps/platform-api/src/db/migrations/. Tracked in schema_migrations(name UNIQUE, checksum, applied_at). Checksum = sha256(file bytes).slice(0,16). Already-applied files MUST be byte-stable or the runner throws a checksum-mismatch and refuses to proceed.
summary.tenantIsolation: Two layers. (1) schema-per-tenant (ADR-0029): per-tenant tenant_settings etc. live in tenant_{uuidv4} schemas applied by the withTenant runner under SET LOCAL search_path. (2) RLS defence-in-depth on public-schema tenant-scoped tables, enforced because the app connects as the non-superuser NOINHERIT role platform_app (ADR-ACT-0189).
summary.rlsBypass: Role-membership based (rls_bypass NOLOGIN role), NOT a user-settable GUC. Canonical bypass predicate (migration 012/023/024+): current_user = 'rls_bypass' (after withSystemAdmin SET LOCAL ROLE) OR (pg_has_role(current_user,'rls_bypass','MEMBER') AND rolinherit(current_user)). platform_app is NOINHERIT so under withTenant() RLS enforces; superuser/pgadmin_sysadmin inherit and bypass.

connectionRoles.POSTGRES_URL: superuser/owner (platform). Used by migrate.ts / seed.ts / reset.ts. Superusers bypass RLS unconditionally.
connectionRoles.POSTGRES_APP_URL: managed platform_app credential (NOSUPERUSER NOINHERIT NOBYPASSRLS LOGIN). Runtime app connection — RLS applies. Also supplies the ${PLATFORM_APP_PASSWORD} materialised into migration 034 at apply-time. Deliberately NO fallback to POSTGRES_URL.
connectionRoles.rls_bypass: NOLOGIN role; SET LOCAL ROLE target for withSystemAdmin(); granted DML + GRANT CREATE ON DATABASE for tenant CREATE SCHEMA
connectionRoles.pgadmin_sysadmin: non-superuser pgAdmin role granted rls_bypass (inherits) — created by docker/postgres/init-extra-databases.sh, grant re-applied idempotently by migration 015

creates: GRANT CREATE ON DATABASE platform TO rls_bypass
bootstrapNonMigration.v2Note: Migrations 008/010/015 re-create/re-grant these idempotently for volumes that pre-date the script, so the script and the migration chain are mutually redundant by design. Keep both in V2.

schemaMigrationsTable.ddl: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations (id SERIAL PK, name TEXT UNIQUE, checksum TEXT, applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now())
schemaMigrationsTable.orderingRule: compareMigrationNames — pure code-point comparison (NOT localeCompare) for locale-stable apply order; filenames are zero-padded ASCII
schemaMigrationsTable.applyUnit: one BEGIN/COMMIT transaction per file; INSERT into schema_migrations inside the same txn; ROLLBACK on failure
schemaMigrationsTable.placeholderMaterialisation: materializeSql() substitutes ${PLATFORM_APP_PASSWORD} from POSTGRES_APP_URL password ONLY; assertSafeRolePassword() rejects quote/backslash/control-char (sole SQL trust boundary). Checksum is over the FILE (placeholder intact) so it is identical across all environments regardless of the real secret.

migrationChain.intent: core identity: users (UNIQUE email), external_identities (UNIQUE provider+subject), organisations (UNIQUE slug), memberships (UNIQUE user+org, role CHECK admin/manager/member/viewer)
PER-TENANT tenant_settings(key PK, value JSONB) + default theme rows. Applied inside tenant_{id} schema by withTenant runner (ADR-0029/0031)
public.tenant_resource_config — per-tenant per-resource tier (shared/dedicated/external/air-gapped) + JSONB config for db/identity/cache/storage
ENABLE+FORCE RLS + tenant_isolation policies on organisations, memberships, tenant_resource_config, users, external_identities (GUC-based bypass — later superseded)
public.platform_config(key PK, value JSONB) quota/capacity governance + default quota rows
DISABLE RLS on organisations (slug->id routing happens pre-session; slug is the public subdomain, not sensitive). Forward-only correction of 004 without editing 004
public.pending_invitations (JIT membership) + partial idx on unconsumed email; public.audit_events + (tenant_id,timestamp DESC) idx

Replace user-settable app.bypass_rls GUC with rls_bypass role-membership check (pg_has_role MEMBER). Creates rls_bypass, grants to app user, rewrites policies on memberships/tenant_resource_config/users/external_identities
public.tenant_auth_settings_credentials — per-tenant realm-admin svc-account client_id + AES-256-GCM client_secret_enc. NO RLS (platform infra secret, withSystemAdmin only)
Create platform_app LOGIN NOSUPERUSER NOBYPASSRLS role (bootstrap password 'platformapppassword'); grant rls_bypass; grant schema privileges. Checksum-frozen in staging/prod — never edit
ALTER ROLE platform_app NOINHERIT; grant DML/seq to rls_bypass; switch policies MEMBER->USAGE (later found insufficient)

CANONICAL bypass predicate: current_user='rls_bypass' OR (MEMBER AND rolinherit). Rewrites memberships/tenant_resource_config/users/external_identities policies. This is the reference predicate reused by 022..031
organisations.parent_id (RESTRICT, single-level via trigger check_suborg_parent_toplevel) + is_active soft-delete + partial parent_id idx (ADR-ACT-0143)
public.vanity_domain_challenges — DNS TXT ownership proof; unique partial idx (domain,org) WHERE unconsumed + verified idx
idempotent GRANT rls_bypass TO pgadmin_sysadmin for volumes pre-dating init script

memberships +username/+status(invited/active/disabled)/+last_login_at/+invited_by; case-insensitive tenant-unique username partial idx; external_identities +email/+last_seen_at; users +status (ADR-ACT-0206)
tenant_auth_settings_credentials +last_validated_at/+last_rotated_at/+rotated_by/+validation_error_kind (no secret, no readiness column — derived live)
public.tenant_email_sender_credentials — AES-256-GCM secret_enc; non-secret config in tenant_settings email.sender. NO RLS (withSystemAdmin)
public.tenant_webhook_subscriptions (secret_enc, event_types[]) + public.tenant_webhook_deliveries (log) + indexes. No RLS, explicit org filter + FK CASCADE
tenant_webhook_deliveries +next_attempt_at/+payload + due idx WHERE status in pending/processing (durable retry/DLQ worker, ADR-0052)
public.tenant_domains — first-class custom-domain lifecycle (ownership/auth_client/routing/tls status, canonical, redirect_policy). routing_*/tls_* only upgraded by live probe (ADR-ACT-0231/0232)
public.tenant_entitlements (UNIQUE org+key, state granted/revoked, deny-by-default). RLS ENABLE+FORCE — but with NAIVE MEMBER predicate (latent leak)
Replace tenant_entitlements policy with canonical current_user+rolinherit predicate (live proof caught the leak). ADR-ACT-0255
public.meter_events (idempotent UNIQUE org+meter+key) + tenant_quotas; canonical RLS. ClickHouse/OpenMeter are Phase-2.5 adapters behind MeteringRepository port (ADR-0067)
public.api_keys (server-gen, scrypt key_hash+salt+pepper, plaintext returned once, UNIQUE key_prefix) + rate-limit policy/counter tables; canonical RLS (ADR-0065)

public.search_documents (tsvector + GIN idx, permission_key gate, UNIQUE org+type+doc_id); canonical RLS. Meilisearch/Typesense/OpenSearch are Phase-4.5 adapters behind SearchIndex/QueryPort (ADR-0060)
public.platform_events outbox (idempotent UNIQUE org+type+key, claim idx) + worker/DLQ tables; canonical RLS. Redis Streams/NATS/Redpanda are Phase-5.5 adapters behind EventBusPort (ADR-0059)
public.user_profiles + notification_preferences + notification log (user_id = IdP subject TEXT, no local FK); canonical RLS. Real transports/Novu Phase-6.5 (ADR-0068)
public.metric_signals + metric_samples + alert_rules + incidents lifecycle; canonical RLS. Prometheus/Tempo/Alertmanager/Grafana-IRM Phase-7.5 (ADR-0062)
public.scheduled_jobs (interval, due idx) enqueuing onto the 027 outbox idempotently per window bucket; canonical RLS (ADR-0059)
public.secret_refs (opaque secret:<uuid> ref, provider builtin AES-256-GCM | openbao backend_path, version, soft revoke); canonical RLS. OpenBao KV v2 is the Tier-1 composed provider (ADR-0069)

public.provider_configs — which concrete provider serves a USF capability per environment; credential_ref BY REFERENCE (CHECK LIKE 'secret:%'), no-forbidden-in-production CHECK; lifecycle candidate->ready. Operator-global, withSystemAdmin (ADR-0070)

public.environment_registry — runtime projection of config/environments/<stage>.json: executor tilt/compose, allowed profiles/mocks, destructive_allowed, data_preservation ephemeral/preserve, secret_store_provider, bootstrap/provider-config status. Operator-global (ADR-0072)

ALTER ROLE platform_app PASSWORD '${PLATFORM_APP_PASSWORD}' — forward-only rotation to the managed credential WITHOUT editing checksum-frozen 010. Placeholder materialised at apply-time; checksum stable (ADR-ACT-0290)

V1C-12c: public.legal_holds (tenant-scoped legal-hold records preventing retention deletion of held resources; set_by/released_by actors; reason; set_at/released_at lifecycle; active = released_at IS NULL). Held-row invariant: an active legal hold on a resource blocks retention candidate deletion (enforced by the retention LegalHoldGuard).
migrationChain.rls: canonical RLS predicate (migration 012) - tenant isolation + rls_bypass

migrationChain.intent: V1C-12b: public.retention_policies (tenant-scoped retention windows per resource class; enabled flag; window duration; soft-disable). Drives the retention tick worker which deletes/anonymises expired candidates UNLESS an active legal_holds row protects the resource. Partial uniqueness migrated to a unique index (V1C-12b fix).
migrationChain.rls: canonical RLS predicate (migration 012) - tenant isolation + rls_bypass

migrationChain.intent: V1C-04: public.delegated_admin_roles (tenant-scoped delegated admin grants; granter+grantee+scope+(optional TTL); soft-delete via revoked_at+revoked_by; partial UNIQUE (grantee, scope) WHERE revoked_at IS NULL; metadata JSONB). Stop condition: held row invariant NOT relevant (this table tracks LID delegations, not legal hold).
migrationChain.rls: canonical RLS predicate (migration 012) — tenant isolation + rls_bypass

migrationChain.intent: V1C-22 slice: public.support_announcements (tenant-scoped announcement log for support/admin comms; subject + message + created_by + created_at; tenant isolation; delivery fans out through the existing notification substrate)

rls.enforcedBy: platform_app is NOSUPERUSER + NOINHERIT; FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on every tenant-scoped public table; canonical bypass predicate
rls.canonicalPredicate: organisation_id::text = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id', true) OR (current_user='rls_bypass' OR (pg_has_role(current_user,'rls_bypass','MEMBER') AND COALESCE((SELECT rolinherit FROM pg_roles WHERE rolname=current_user LIMIT 1),false)))
rls.sessionContext: withTenant() sets SET LOCAL app.current_tenant_id; withSystemAdmin() does SET LOCAL ROLE rls_bypass — both transaction-scoped, pool-safe
rls.regressionGuard: live proof scripts (e.g. proof:entitlements-postgres) assert an unfiltered count under another tenant context returns 0 — caught the 022 naive-predicate leak

perTenantSchema.model: ADR-0029 schema-per-tenant; ADR-0031 withTenant migration runner
perTenantSchema.tenantSchemaName: tenant_{organisation_id} where organisation_id MUST be UUID v4 (drives tenantSchemaIdentifier)
perTenantSchema.note: Public-schema USF tables (024..031) are tenant-scoped by RLS column, NOT by per-tenant schema — only tenant_settings currently lives in the per-tenant schema.

redis.role: cache, rate-limit counters (redis-rate-limit-repository — Phase-3.5 provider behind RateLimitRepository port), session/forward-auth, event-bus provider candidate (Phase-5.5)
redis.schemaOwnership: NONE — no migration owns Redis; keys are runtime-managed. Redis is a provider adapter, never canonical state.
redis.retention: ephemeral semantics; AOF for crash recovery only. Local flush via make redis-flush-local.
redis.v2: Reuse as-is. No data migration needed (rebuildable cache/counters). Fresh-install = empty volume.

clickhouse.role: Phase-2.5 metering analytics provider behind MeteringRepository port (ADR-0067). Canonical metering is Postgres meter_events; ClickHouse is an optional analytical mirror.
clickhouse.schemaOwnership: NONE in the platform migration chain. ClickHouse tables (if/when the provider is activated) are owned by the ClickHouse adapter, not migrate.ts.
clickhouse.retention: analytical/long-window (provider-defined). Not the source of truth.
clickhouse.v2: Reuse image+volume as-is. No platform-DB migration interplay. Fresh-install = empty until the metering provider is configured via provider_configs.

minio.role: object storage; OIDC-integrated (Keycloak minio client, ADR-0073 claim->policy mapping). storage_tier in tenant_resource_config.
minio.schemaOwnership: NONE — buckets/objects are runtime-managed, no SQL migration.
minio.retention: durable object data; bucket lifecycle policies are provider-side.
minio.v2: Reuse as-is; retain the minio-data volume on upgrade (real customer objects). Fresh-install = empty buckets provisioned at first use.

fixtureConstants.roles: admin=tenant-admin, viewer, manager, member (forbidden has no membership)

idempotencyGotchas: DELETE FROM organisations WHERE slug=fixture-org AND id<>v4id first — reconciles a legacy non-v4 fixture id that would collide on slug UNIQUE (the v4 correction).
users insert uses bare ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (covers BOTH id PK and email UNIQUE) — a persona that logged in under its real Keycloak sub must not crash boot-time re-seed.
memberships resolved by JOIN on email (not hardcoded fixture id) — a persona created by login has its real id, so a hardcoded-id insert would hit memberships_user_id_fkey.
seedsAndFixtures.v2: Reuse fixture model verbatim — it is the semantic source for V2 E2E personas/journeys (per governing intent). Keep the v4 ORG_ID and email-join idempotency. Fixtures are dev/test only; production never seeds fixtures.

backup.behaviour: pg_dump | gzip to .local-artifacts/backups (git-ignored, umask 077 + chmod 600 owner-only)
backup.guard: ENV outside dev|test REFUSED unless ALLOW_BACKUP_ENV=<ENV> explicitly set — never quietly dumps a shared DB

restore.behaviour: destructive overwrite of target DB from a .sql.gz
restore.guard: refuses unless ENV in {dev,test} AND CONFIRM_RESTORE=restore-<ENV> — intentionally hard to fire by accident

backupRestore.scope: Postgres only. Redis (rebuildable), ClickHouse (analytical mirror), MinIO (provider-native lifecycle) have no platform backup script — they are not canonical state.
backupRestore.v2: Reuse both scripts unchanged. They already encode the destructive-op controls V2 needs.

destructiveOpControls.resetTs: apps/platform-api/src/db/reset.ts — drops memberships/external_identities/users/organisations/schema_migrations CASCADE; REFUSES unless NODE_ENV in {development,test,local}. Note: only drops the original 001 identity tables, NOT the later USF tables — a true full reset relies on dropping the volume.
destructiveOpControls.checksumImmutability: migrate.ts throws on any checksum mismatch for an applied file — committed migrations are effectively immutable; corrections are forward-only (006 corrects 004; 023 corrects 022; 034 corrects 010 without editing it).
destructiveOpControls.environmentRegistry: environment_registry.destructive_allowed (default false) + data_preservation (ephemeral|preserve) gate destructive ops per stage (ADR-0072). Mocks forbidden in staging/prod (mock_policy no-mocks).
destructiveOpControls.secretSafety: platform_app password never hardcoded in a managed-secret migration (Sonar S6698); materialized from POSTGRES_APP_URL at apply-time with assertSafeRolePassword() trust boundary.

dataVerification.isMigrated: isMigrated() — COUNT(schema_migrations)>0, used as a precondition by seedFixtures
dataVerification.checksumVerify: every run re-hashes each file and compares to stored checksum (drift detection)
dataVerification.liveProofs: proof scripts assert RLS isolation (cross-tenant unfiltered count == 0), readiness derived from live probes never stored columns (017/021), idempotent publish/meter invariants
dataVerification.v2: Reuse the checksum+isMigrated gate and the RLS live-proof pattern as V2 data-verification acceptance gates.

squashedFreshInstallBaseline.decision: NO squash for V2. Keep the full 001..034 chain as the V2 fresh-install baseline.
rationale: Checksums of applied migrations are immutable in long-lived staging/prod; a squashed 001 would have a different checksum than the recorded rows and brick migrate.ts (exactly the ADR-ACT-0290 failure mode for editing 010).
Forward-only corrections (006/023/034) carry load-bearing provenance and ADR linkage; squashing erases the audit trail the governing intent wants preserved.
A fresh V2 install applies all 34 files in code-point order in seconds; there is no performance reason to squash.
Per governing intent the final tree must have no transitional naming — but migration numbers 001..034 are canonical history, not transitional artefacts, so they stay.
squashedFreshInstallBaseline.alternativeRejected: An optional consolidated baseline-snapshot.sql for brand-new envs was considered and rejected: it creates a second source of truth that must be kept byte-identical to the chain forever.

v1ToV2UpgradePath.decision: In-place forward migration. An existing V1 install IS a V2 install at the data layer — V2 reuses the identical Postgres schema, roles, RLS, and migration chain.
steps: 1. V2 ships the same migrations/ directory (001..034) plus any new V2-only forward migrations appended as 035+.
2. On deploy, migrate.ts skips all already-applied rows (checksum match) and applies only the new tail — zero data movement for 001..034.
3. platform_app password already rotated by 034; POSTGRES_APP_URL unchanged.
4. Per-tenant schemas (tenant_{v4}) and their tenant_settings are untouched.
5. External volumes (redis/clickhouse/minio) are reattached as-is — no migration.
v1ToV2UpgradePath.noDestructiveStep: No DROP, no reseed, no volume recreation on a real upgrade. Fixtures are not seeded in production.

historicalMigrationProvenance.decision: All 34 historical migration files remain in apps/platform-api/src/db/migrations/ verbatim, with their leading ADR-reference comment blocks intact.
historicalMigrationProvenance.rationale: They are the byte-stable inputs to the checksum invariant AND the human-readable decision lineage (each cites its ADR and explains why a later file corrects an earlier one). Removing or renaming any is forbidden — it would break both checksum continuity and provenance.

checksumPreservation.decision: Preserve the sha256-first-16-hex-of-file-bytes scheme and the placeholder-aware materialisation exactly.
invariants: checksum computed over committed file bytes (placeholder intact), never over materialized SQL
applied files must remain byte-for-byte identical forever
secrets enter only via ${PLATFORM_APP_PASSWORD} materialised at apply-time from POSTGRES_APP_URL
corrections are new forward files, never edits

externalVolumeMigrationRetention.postgres-data: RETAIN on upgrade (canonical state). Fresh install = empty + full migrate + (dev/test only) seed.
externalVolumeMigrationRetention.redis-data: RETAIN or DISCARD freely (rebuildable cache/counters/AOF). No correctness dependency.
externalVolumeMigrationRetention.clickhouse-data: RETAIN if metering analytics provider is active; otherwise empty. Not source of truth.
externalVolumeMigrationRetention.minio-data: RETAIN on upgrade (real objects). Fresh install = empty.

dependencyStackVolumes.note: Not platform product data — retained as the dependency stack requires, never migrated by the platform.

steps: compose up default profile (postgres redis clickhouse minio mailpit otel-collector) — fresh named volumes
postgres initdb runs docker/postgres/init-extra-databases.sh (rls_bypass, pgadmin_sysadmin, grants)
npm run db:migrate (migrate.ts) applies 001..034 in code-point order, materialising ${PLATFORM_APP_PASSWORD} from POSTGRES_APP_URL
(dev/test only) npm run db:seed (seedFixtures) creates the fixture-org + 5 personas
redis/clickhouse/minio start empty; providers activated lazily via provider_configs / tenant_resource_config
verification: isMigrated()===true, RLS live-proof cross-tenant count==0
v2FreshInstallBaseline.noState: No production data; per-tenant schemas created on demand by withSystemAdmin CREATE SCHEMA during tenant provisioning.

rollbackPosition.policy: Forward-only. There are no down-migrations and migrate.ts has no rollback path by design.
mechanisms: Per-file BEGIN/COMMIT: a failing migration ROLLBACKs its own transaction and is never recorded — safe to fix-forward and re-run.
To revert a deployed schema change, ship a new forward migration (the 006/023/034 pattern), never edit history.
Disaster recovery: restore from a Postgres backup (scripts/backup/postgres-restore.sh, dev/test) or stage-equivalent managed snapshot.
environment_registry.data_preservation gates whether a stage may be torn down at all.

v2Actions: Carry migrations/ 001..034 verbatim into V2; append V2-only changes as 035+ forward files.
Keep migrate.ts checksum + placeholder-materialisation + code-point ordering unchanged.
Keep the canonical RLS bypass predicate and platform_app NOINHERIT enforcement; add the canonical predicate to every new tenant-scoped table (never the naive MEMBER form — see 022 leak).
Reuse seed.ts fixture model as the V2 E2E persona source.
Reuse backup/restore scripts and their guards unchanged.
Reuse redis/clickhouse/minio as provider adapters behind ports — never as canonical schema owners.
Consider hardening reset.ts to drop ALL platform tables (currently only the 001 identity tables) OR document that full reset = drop volume — dev/test only.
